steps.title: Track the prediction
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that immature males should share fewer maternal food preferences than females do because males disperse. Individual 17 is the male comparison point, and individual 5 is the female comparison point.

steps.title: Use both graph measures
steps.content: Support must point in the same direction on both measures: individual 17 should have a smaller percentage of its mother's diet shared with it and a larger percentage of its own diet not shared with its mother, compared with individual 5. That uses the graph's two different percentages, not just one overlap number.

steps.title: Reject partial descriptions
steps.content: A and D report only the mother-diet overlap measure, so they leave out the unshared-offspring-diet measure. C reports values without showing the predicted male-female contrast. The option that compares both measures in the predicted direction is needed. The answer is B.
